questions.question: According to the lecture (Slide 5): which is accurate?
options:
Acute Otitis Media
Pathophysiology
Inflammation of the middle ear
Structures lined by mucous membranes
Mastoid...
This applies only to adult patients.
This is not a standard consideration.
This is contradicted by current guidelines.
questions.rationale:
Slide 5: Acute Otitis Media
Pathophysiology
Inflammation of the middle ear
Structures lined by mucous membranes
Mastoid sinuses
Middle ear
Eustachian tube
Protects middle ear
Provides drainage
Equalizes air pressure
Infection of the throat can easily spread to the middle ear and mastoid

questions.question: According to the lecture (Slide 6): which is accurate?
options:
Hearing Impairment 
Hearing loss can result from 
Defects in the transmission of sound to the middle ear
Damag...
This applies only to adult patients.
This is not a standard consideration.
This is contradicted by current guidelines.
questions.rationale:
Slide 6: Hearing Impairment 
Hearing loss can result from 
Defects in the transmission of sound to the middle ear
Damage to the auditory nerve or ear structures
A mixed loss involving both a defect in nerve pathways and interference with sound transmission
Behavior problems may arise because these children do not understand verbal directions

questions.question: According to the lecture (Slide 11): which is accurate?
options:
Strabismus
Also known as cross-eye
Child is not able to direct both eyes in same direction
Lack of coordinatio...
This applies only to adult patients.
This is not a standard consideration.
This is contradicted by current guidelines.
questions.rationale:
Slide 11: Strabismus
Also known as cross-eye
Child is not able to direct both eyes in same direction
Lack of coordination between the eye muscles that direct movement of the eyes
When coordination does not occur, the brain will disable one eye to provide a clear image

questions.question: According to the lecture (Slide 14): which is accurate?
options:
Hyphema
Presence of blood in the anterior chamber of the eye
One of the most common ocular injuries
Appears as...
This applies only to adult patients.
This is not a standard consideration.
This is contradicted by current guidelines.
questions.rationale:
Slide 14: Hyphema
Presence of blood in the anterior chamber of the eye
One of the most common ocular injuries
Appears as a bright-red or dark-red spot in front of the lower portion of the iris
Treatment
Bedrest with HOB elevated 30 to 45 degrees decreases intraocular pressure and intracranial pressure if there is an associated head injury
